pdn.desc: Draft Plan of Condominium for 70 residential units

Proposed construction of a 29-storey residential tower fronting Jarvis Street including just under 15,000sqm of new gross floor area. In total, the maximum height of the proposed building is 99.0m, including a three-storey podium and 7m mechanical penthouse. In total, this represents a Floor Space Index of 11.70. The proposed unit mix includes 26 Studios (12%), 123 one-bedroom/one+den (58%), 42 two-bedroom/two+den (20%) and 22 three-bedroom/three+den (10%) units.

Site Plan Control Application to permit a 25-storey, mixed use building with 300 dwelling units, 20,974.8 square metres of residential gross floor area, 710 square metres of non-residential gross floor area, 97 parking spaces and 308 bicycle parking spaces.   The proposal has been revised, from the original submission which was 23-storey residential building with 128 dwelling units, 9,073 square metres of residential gross floor area, 37 parking spaces and 142 bicycle parking spaces.

Zoning By-law Amendment application to permit a 64-storey mixed-use building, containing 514 dwelling units and 1,000 square metres of non-residential gross floor area. The proposal includes heritage conservation on the site.

OPA & Rezoning Application proposes the development of an 38-storey mixed-use building providing rental residential and retail use at grade. The building will provide a total of 399 residential units, being a mix of studio, 1-bedroom, 2-bedroom, and 3-bedroom units. The building will also provide one retail unit at grade. A paved access from Hendon Ave will be provided, leading to 3 levels of below-grade parking, providing a total of 69 parking spaces; as well as 310 bicycle parking spaces. The proposed development provides a total GFA of 27,841.90 square metres and a FSI of 15.37. Additionally, the proposed development provides 518.70 square metres of indoor amenity space and 598.50 square metres of outdoor amenity space.

Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment applications to permit three new buildings on the lands, comprising a 58-storey mixed-use condominium building with 4,060 square metres of non-residential uses in the lower floors and 741 dwelling units, a 21-storey purpose built rental building with 287 dwelling units with 310 square metres of community space, and a 9-storey purpose built rental building with 81 dwelling units. The proposal also includes 18 replacement rental units, of which the exact location remains to be determined.   A 3,135 square metre parkland conveyance plus a 772 square metre Stratified Public Park Area and enhanced open spaces with a range of programming across the site are proposed.    A new public street, which would extend Rose Avenue through to the northern boundary of the site and a series of private streets across the site are proposed.     The proposal includes modifications to the layout and uses within the existing buildings to create new amenity and non-residential spaces, which would result in the loss of 18 existing rental dwelling units that would be replaced elsewhere within the site as determined though the development review. The remaining 2,251 rental dwelling units are proposed to be retained, along with 1,828 square metres of existing non-residential space. The existing and proposed buildings would result in 3,360 dwelling units with a gross floor area of 231,028 square metres, and 6,198 square metres of non-residential gross floor area, for a total gross floor area of 237,226 square metres across the site.

Zoning By-law Amendment application for the lands at 1-70 Eglinton Square, 1431 & 1437 Victoria Park Ave, 14, 18, 22 and 26 Engelhart Cres within the Golden Mile Secondary Plan Area to permit a mixed-use development. The initial proposal was for two towers measuring 24 storeys (90 Eglinton Ave W) and 32 storeys (50-60 Eglinton Ave W) in height. The proposal has now been amended to increase the height of the towers to 34 and 39 storeys in height atop a 3-storey mixed-use base building with 688 residential units. The revised re-zoning and Official Plan amendment applications propose a 6-storey (19.65 m) residential building consisting of 4 two-storey grade-related townhouse units and 67 apartment units, for a total of 71 units. Site plan approval o facilitate the redevelopment of the site with a 59-Storey (west tower) and a 48-Storey (east tower) mixed-use buildings, that will include: affordable rental units, S111 replacement units, community spaces, community retail, a daycare facility, on-site parkland dedication, as well as 4 levels of underground parking.

The application proposes to increase the permitted height and density from the approved heights of 35 and 37-storeys as per By-law 1263-2022 (OLT), to 46 and 58-storeys This height increase would result in a total gross floor area of 73,305 m2, comprised of 71,992 m2 of residential GFA and 1,313 m2 of non-residential GFA at-grade. A total of 1,206 residential units are proposed, including 553 units in the west tower and 653 in the east tower. Vehicular and loading access to the development continues to be proposed from the future east-west private laneway on-site, connecting Henning Avenue and Duplex Avenue. Three (3) levels of underground parking are proposed, containing a total of 216 vehicular parking spaces. The revised buildings maintain the setbacks, stepbacks, commercial uses, and heritage retention of the previous approval.
